message:
fix(packs): simplify restic patterns and expand git branch coverage

## Restic Pattern Simplification
Simplified restic destructive patterns from complex nested optional groups
to straightforward `\b.*\s` anchored patterns. The previous patterns like
`restic(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+forget\b` were overly complex and could
miss edge cases with unusual flag ordering.

New patterns use simpler form: `restic\b.*\sforget\b`
- More permissive matching covers all flag orderings
- Word boundary `\b` prevents false matches on similar command names
- `.*\s` allows any intervening flags/arguments

Affected patterns:
- restic-forget
- restic-prune
- restic-key-remove
- restic-unlock-remove-all
- restic-cache-cleanup

## Git Branch Pattern Expansion
Extended git branch force delete detection to cover all force variants:
- `git branch -D` (original)
- `git branch --force` (long form)
- `git branch -f` (short form)

All three flags allow overwriting or deleting branches without safety checks.
Updated pattern from `-D\b` to `(?:-D\b|--force\b|-f\b)`.

Updated related suggestion key from `branch-force-delete` to `branch-force`
to reflect the broader scope.

## Test Corpus Updates
Added new test cases for git branch force variants in
tests/corpus/true_positives/git_destructive.toml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -50,27 +50,27 @@ fn create_destructive_patterns() -> Vec<DestructivePattern> {
     vec![
         destructive_pattern!(
             "restic-forget",
-            r"restic(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+forget\b",
+            r"restic\b.*\sforget\b",
             "restic forget removes snapshots and can permanently delete backup data."
         ),
         destructive_pattern!(
             "restic-prune",
-            r"restic(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+prune\b",
+            r"restic\b.*\sprune\b",
             "restic prune removes unreferenced data and is irreversible."
         ),
         destructive_pattern!(
             "restic-key-remove",
-            r"restic(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+key(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+remove\b",
+            r"restic\b.*\skey\b.*\sremove\b",
             "restic key remove deletes encryption keys and can make backups unrecoverable."
         ),
         destructive_pattern!(
             "restic-unlock-remove-all",
-            r"restic(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+unlock\b(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+--remove-all\b",
+            r"restic\b.*\sunlock\b.*\s--remove-all\b",
             "restic unlock --remove-all force-removes repository locks."
         ),
         destructive_pattern!(
             "restic-cache-cleanup",
-            r"restic(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+cache\b(?:\s+--?\S+(?:\s+\S+)?)*\s+--cleanup\b",
+            r"restic\b.*\scache\b.*\s--cleanup\b",
             "restic cache --cleanup removes cached data from disk."
         ),
     ]

@@ -245,7 +245,7 @@ fn register_core_git_suggestions(m: &mut HashMap<&'static str, Vec<Suggestion>>)
     );
 
     m.insert(
-        "core.git:branch-force-delete",
+        "core.git:branch-force",
         vec![
             Suggestion::new(
                 SuggestionKind::PreviewFirst,
@@ -1086,7 +1086,7 @@ mod tests {
             "core.git:push-force-short",
             "core.git:checkout-discard",
             "core.git:checkout-ref-discard",
-            "core.git:branch-force-delete",
+            "core.git:branch-force",
             "core.git:restore-worktree",
             "core.git:restore-worktree-explicit",
             "core.git:stash-drop",

@@ -57,7 +57,7 @@ rule_id = "core.git:stash-clear"
 description = "git branch -D force deletes"
 command = "git branch -D feature-branch"
 expected = "deny"
-rule_id = "core.git:branch-force-delete"
+rule_id = "core.git:branch-force"
 
 [[case]]
 description = "git push --force"
